rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 10, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1 Plaintiffs motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiffs have leave to file a First Amended Complaint for Survival, Wrongful Death, including leave to add two existing defendants (Chevron Phillips Chemical Company LP and Atlantic Richfield Company) to the First through Fourth causes of action and to remove those same defendants from the Eighth and Ninth causes of action, as identified in the Notice of Motion. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 10, 2018 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' demurrer to defendant Union Carbide Corp.'s answer to plaintiffs' complaint is overruled for the following reasons: Plaintiffs' demurrer violates CRC 3.1320(a) because it does not state each ground of demurrer in a separate paragraph. The Court will not consider plaintiffs' argument based on C.C.P. § 431.30(g) as they did not identify that as a ground for demurrer in their Notice of Demurrer and Demurrer. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate how the forty-five affirmative defenses to which they demur do not state facts sufficient to constitute a defense or are uncertain. (C.C.P. § 430.20(a), (b).) Plaintiffs failed to meet and confer prior to filing their demurrer. (C.C.P. § 430.41.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
